title: Introduction
content:
## Purpose and Scope

This Digital Adaptation Kit (DAK) provides comprehensive guidance for implementing the **Core Information Standard v2.0** across digital health systems. The standard establishes a foundational framework for capturing, storing, and sharing essential person-centered information in health and care settings.

The Core Information Standard serves as a universal baseline for patient information management, ensuring that critical data elements are consistently recorded and accessible across the care continuum. This DAK translates the standard's requirements into actionable technical specifications for system developers, implementers, and healthcare organizations.

## Background and Context

### Health Domain Overview

The fragmentation of health information systems represents a significant challenge to delivering coordinated, person-centered care. Studies indicate that incomplete or inaccessible patient information contributes to approximately 10% of patient safety incidents and significantly impacts care quality and efficiency.

In the UK context, where this standard originates, the NHS serves over 66 million people with care delivered across multiple settings - from GP practices and community services to acute hospitals and social care providers. The lack of standardized information exchange between these settings creates barriers to continuity of care, particularly for individuals with complex needs who interact with multiple services.

### Policy Context

The Core Information Standard aligns with several key policy initiatives:

- **NHS Long Term Plan**: Emphasizes the need for interoperable digital systems that support integrated care
- **Data Strategy for Health and Social Care**: Promotes standardized data capture and sharing to improve outcomes
- **Person-Centered Care Framework**: Ensures information systems support individual preferences and needs

## Relationship to Clinical Guidelines

While the Core Information Standard is not a clinical guideline itself, it provides the informational foundation that enables adherence to clinical best practices. It ensures that:

1. **Essential demographic and contact information** is available for patient identification and communication
2. **Clinical history and current health status** can be accurately documented and shared
3. **Person preferences and requirements** are captured to support individualized care delivery
4. **Legal and safeguarding information** is recorded to ensure appropriate protections

## Document Overview

This DAK is structured according to WHO SMART Guidelines methodology, providing:

- **Health interventions and recommendations** derived from the standard's requirements
- **Generic personas** representing key user groups
- **User scenarios** illustrating practical applications
- **Business processes and workflows** for implementation
- **Core data elements** with detailed specifications
- **Decision support logic** for data validation and clinical rules
- **Indicators and metrics** for monitoring implementation
- **Functional and non-functional requirements** for system design
- **Implementation references and resources**

title: Health Interventions & Recommendations
content:
## Overview

The Core Information Standard v2.0 defines essential information management interventions that support safe, effective, and person-centered care delivery. These interventions focus on systematic capture, maintenance, and sharing of core patient information across health and care settings.

## Key Interventions Summary

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source/Guideline | Evidence Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Comprehensive Identity Verification** | Verify patient identity using NHS number and minimum two demographic identifiers at every care interaction | NHS Patient Safety Standards, NICE Guidelines | A - Strong recommendation |
| **Reasonable Adjustments Assessment** | Assess and document all reasonable adjustments required under Equality Act 2010 within 24 hours of first contact | Equality Act 2010, NHS Accessible Information Standard | A - Mandatory requirement |
| **Safeguarding Screening** | Screen all patients for safeguarding concerns using validated assessment tools | NHS Safeguarding Policy, Working Together 2018 | A - Strong recommendation |
| **Medication Reconciliation** | Complete medication reconciliation within 24 hours of admission and at all care transitions | NICE NG5, WHO High 5s | A - Strong recommendation |
| **Allergy and Adverse Reaction Documentation** | Document all known allergies and adverse reactions with severity and certainty indicators | NICE CG183, NHS Patient Safety Alert | A - Strong recommendation |
| **Care Plan Development** | Develop person-centered care plans with patient/representative involvement within 48 hours | NICE QS13, Person-Centered Care Guidelines | B - Moderate recommendation |
| **Legal Status Documentation** | Document mental capacity, consent status, and advance decisions at admission and when status changes | Mental Capacity Act 2005, NICE NG108 | A - Mandatory requirement |
| **Contact Information Validation** | Validate and update contact information at every scheduled encounter | Information Governance Standards | B - Moderate recommendation |

## Detailed Intervention Descriptions

### Identity Management Interventions

The standard mandates robust identity verification processes to prevent patient misidentification - a never event in healthcare. Key requirements include:

- **Primary identifier**: NHS number (or equivalent national identifier) must be verified against national systems
- **Demographic matching**: Minimum of name, date of birth, and address must be confirmed
- **Biometric options**: Photograph capture recommended for enhanced identification

### Clinical Information Interventions

#### Problem List Maintenance
- Maintain an accurate, coded problem list using SNOMED CT terminology
- Review and update at every clinical encounter
- Include both active and resolved conditions with appropriate status indicators

#### Medication Management
- Document all current medications including over-the-counter and complementary therapies
- Use dm+d coding for all prescribed items
- Include dose, route, frequency, and indication for each medication
- Record medication allergies and intolerances with reaction details

#### Assessment Documentation
- Conduct and document standardized assessments for:
  - Cognitive function (where indicated)
  - Mobility and functional status
  - Social circumstances and support needs
  - Mental health and wellbeing

### Person-Centered Interventions

#### "About Me" Documentation
- Capture patient preferences, values, and what matters most to them
- Document communication needs and preferences
- Record how the person wishes to be supported
- Update regularly through patient/carer involvement

#### Reasonable Adjustments
- Systematically identify needs for:
  - Communication support (e.g., interpreters, easy-read materials)
  - Physical adjustments (e.g., accessible facilities, longer appointments)
  - Sensory adjustments (e.g., hearing loops, large print)
- Flag adjustments prominently in clinical systems

### Safeguarding Interventions

- Screen for safeguarding risks including:
  - Domestic abuse
  - Child protection concerns
  - Adult safeguarding needs
  - Mental health crisis indicators
- Document with appropriate confidentiality controls
- Establish clear escalation pathways

### Legal and Ethical Interventions

#### Consent Management
- Document informed consent for information sharing
- Record treatment preferences and limitations
- Capture advance decisions and living wills
- Identify appointed decision-makers (LPA, deputies)

#### Mental Capacity Assessment
- Assess and document capacity for specific decisions
- Record best interest decisions where capacity is lacking
- Review capacity status regularly as conditions change

## Implementation Considerations

Successful implementation requires:
- Staff training on all intervention protocols
- Clinical decision support tools embedded in workflows
- Regular audit and feedback on compliance
- Patient and carer engagement in design and testing
- Phased rollout with continuous improvement cycles

title: Generic Personas
content:
## Overview

The following personas represent key user groups who interact with the Core Information Standard. Each persona captures typical characteristics, responsibilities, and system needs to guide implementation design.

## Persona Summary Table

| Persona ID | Occupational Title | Description | Targeted | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| P001 | General Practitioner (GP) | Primary care physician managing patient lists, providing first-contact care | Yes | Clinical assessment, care coordination, prescribing, health promotion |
| P002 | Practice Nurse | Registered nurse in primary care supporting chronic disease management | Yes | Clinical procedures, patient education, care planning, immunizations |
| P003 | Hospital Doctor | Secondary care physician providing specialized inpatient/outpatient care | Yes | Specialist diagnostics, acute care management, discharge planning |
| P004 | Ward Nurse | Registered nurse providing 24/7 inpatient care on hospital wards | Yes | Medication administration, care delivery, patient monitoring |
| P005 | Social Worker | Professional supporting vulnerable individuals with social care needs | Yes | Safeguarding assessment, care coordination, advocacy |
| P006 | Community Nurse | Registered nurse providing care in patients' homes and community settings | Yes | Independent clinical practice, care management, health education |
| P007 | Care Home Manager | Manager overseeing residential care facility operations | Yes | Care quality oversight, regulatory compliance, staff management |
| P008 | Patient/Person | Individual receiving health and social care services | Yes | Self-management, decision-making, communication of needs |
| P009 | Informal Carer | Family member or friend providing unpaid care support | Yes | Personal care delivery, advocacy, care coordination |
| P010 | Administrative Staff | Non-clinical staff supporting service operations | Yes | Data entry, appointment management, records management |

## Detailed Persona Descriptions

### P001: General Practitioner (GP)

**Dr. Sarah Chen** is an experienced GP partner in a medium-sized practice serving 12,000 patients. She sees 30-40 patients daily across face-to-face and remote consultations.

**Key Responsibilities:**
- First-contact assessment and diagnosis
- Ongoing management of chronic conditions
- Preventive care and health screening
- Referral to secondary care services
- Medication review and prescribing

**Information Needs:**
- Quick access to comprehensive patient history
- Real-time medication and allergy information
- Hospital discharge summaries and specialist letters
- Safeguarding alerts and care plans
- Ability to update problem lists and care plans efficiently

**System Interaction:**
- Primary user of read/write functions
- Requires rapid data entry during 10-minute consultations
- Needs clinical decision support for prescribing and referrals

### P002: Practice Nurse

**James Thompson** is a practice nurse specializing in diabetes and respiratory care, conducting structured chronic disease reviews.

**Key Responsibilities:**
- Chronic disease monitoring and management
- Immunization delivery
- Health assessments and screening
- Patient education and self-management support
- Care plan development and review

**Information Needs:**
- Disease-specific templates and protocols
- Historical test results and trends
- Care plan goals and progress tracking
- Patient preference and adjustment information

**System Interaction:**
- Heavy user of structured data entry forms
- Requires integration with point-of-care devices
- Needs automated recall and reminder systems

### P003: Hospital Doctor

**Mr. Ahmed Patel** is a consultant physician in elderly care medicine, managing complex multi-morbid patients.

**Key Responsibilities:**
- Comprehensive geriatric assessment
- Acute medical management
- Discharge planning and coordination
- Multidisciplinary team leadership

**Information Needs:**
- GP referral information and community care context
- Complete medication history from all sources
- Functional and cognitive assessment results
- Social circumstances and support networks
- Advance care planning documentation

**System Interaction:**
- Requires mobile access for ward rounds
- Needs to view information from multiple sources
- Must document complex assessments efficiently

### P004: Ward Nurse

**Sister Mary O'Brien** leads a busy medical ward team providing 24/7 care for acutely ill patients.

**Key Responsibilities:**
- Medication administration and monitoring
- Personal care coordination
- Patient observation and assessment
- Discharge preparation
- Family communication

**Information Needs:**
- Real-time care plans and task lists
- Allergy and risk alerts
- Reasonable adjustment requirements
- Contact details for next of kin
- Discharge planning information

**System Interaction:**
- Uses bedside terminals and mobile devices
- Requires quick documentation during care delivery
- Needs clear visual alerts and warnings

### P005: Social Worker

**Rebecca Williams** is a senior social worker in the integrated discharge team, coordinating complex discharges.

**Key Responsibilities:**
- Social care assessment
- Safeguarding investigations
- Care package arrangement
- Mental capacity assessment
- Multi-agency coordination

**Information Needs:**
- Comprehensive social history
- Legal status and capacity information
- Family dynamics and support systems
- Financial and housing circumstances
- Previous care packages and outcomes

**System Interaction:**
- Requires access from multiple locations
- Needs to share information with external agencies
- Must maintain detailed case notes

### P006: Community Nurse

**David Lee** is a district nurse providing complex care in patients' homes across a rural area.

**Key Responsibilities:**
- Wound care and clinical procedures
- Medication support and administration
- End-of-life care
- Health education and promotion
- Care coordination with GP practices

**Information Needs:**
- Mobile access to care plans
- Wound assessment photos and progress
- Equipment and supply requirements
- Home environment risks and access codes
- Family carer capability and training needs

**System Interaction:**
- Primarily mobile/tablet user
- Works offline with synchronization
- Needs voice input options

### P007: Care Home Manager

**Patricia Brown** manages a 60-bed residential care home for older adults with dementia.

**Key Responsibilities:**
- Care quality oversight
- Regulatory compliance
- Staff supervision and training
- Family liaison
- Health service coordination

**Information Needs:**
- Resident care plans and risk assessments
- Medication administration records
- Incident reports and safeguarding
- Staff training records
- GP visit records and hospital communications

**System Interaction:**
- Oversees data quality and completeness
- Generates regulatory reports
- Manages access permissions for staff

### P008: Patient/Person

**Mrs. Ethel Johnson**, 78, lives alone with multiple chronic conditions including diabetes, heart failure, and mild cognitive impairment.

**Key Responsibilities:**
- Self-management of health conditions
- Attending appointments
- Communicating symptoms and concerns
- Making informed decisions
- Managing medications

**Information Needs:**
- Understanding of conditions and treatments
- Appointment schedules and reminders
- Medication instructions
- Test results in plain language
- Care team contact information

**System Interaction:**
- May use patient portal or app
- Requires simple, accessible interfaces
- Needs large print/audio options

### P009: Informal Carer

**Michael Johnson** cares for his mother Ethel, managing her appointments and providing daily support.

**Key Responsibilities:**
- Medication prompting and management
- Appointment coordination
- Personal care assistance
- Advocacy and communication
- Emergency response

**Information Needs:**
- Care plan and daily routines
- Medication schedules and changes
- Emergency contact information
- Appointment notifications
- Education about conditions

**System Interaction:**
- Proxy access to relevant information
- Mobile notifications and alerts
- Ability to update observations

### P010: Administrative Staff

**Jennifer Clark** is a medical secretary supporting clinical teams with administrative tasks.

**Key Responsibilities:**
- Appointment scheduling
- Data entry and record maintenance
- Correspondence management
- Report generation
- Patient registration

**Information Needs:**
- Patient demographic information
- Appointment history and preferences
- Contact details and communication needs
- Reasonable adjustment requirements

**System Interaction:**
- High-volume data entry
- Requires efficient search functions
- Needs bulk update capabilities

title: User Scenarios
content:
## Overview

These user scenarios illustrate how different personas interact with the Core Information Standard in real-world situations. Each scenario demonstrates key workflows, data requirements, and system interactions.

## Scenario 1: Emergency Hospital Admission

### Title: Elderly Patient with Complex Needs Admitted via Emergency Department

**Context:** Mrs. Ethel Johnson (78) is brought to the Emergency Department after a fall at home. She has mild cognitive impairment and multiple chronic conditions.

**Narrative:**
The paramedics arrive at the ED with Mrs. Johnson at 14:30 on a Tuesday afternoon. She is confused and unable to provide a clear history. The triage nurse, using a tablet device, searches for her record using her name and date of birth. The system immediately flags several critical alerts:
- Penicillin allergy with previous anaphylaxis
- Cognitive impairment requiring simple language
- Son Michael is primary contact and holds Lasting Power of Attorney
- High falls risk with previous fractures
- Current medications include warfarin with recent INR of 3.2

The ED consultant accesses her GP summary, noting recent increasing confusion reported by the district nurse. Her 'About Me' section indicates she becomes distressed in noisy environments and responds better to a calm, slow approach. The team moves her to a quiet side room and contacts her son.

**Data Elements Utilized:**

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Identity | NHS number, name, DOB, photograph for identification |
| Clinical | Allergy status, current medications, recent test results, problem list |
| Safeguarding | Falls risk assessment, vulnerability indicators |
| Legal | LPA status, capacity assessment from GP |
| Personal | Communication preferences, environmental needs |
| Contacts | Next of kin, GP details, community nurse information |

**Personas Involved:**
- P004: Ward Nurse (triage and initial assessment)
- P003: Hospital Doctor (clinical decision-making)
- P009: Informal Carer (providing collateral history)
- P001: GP (accessed records)

**Related Business Processes:**
- BP01: Patient Registration and Verification
- BP02: Clinical Assessment and Documentation
- BP03: Safeguarding Screening
- BP04: Care Coordination

## Scenario 2: Planned Care Home Admission

### Title: Social Care Assessment and Care Home Placement

**Context:** Mr. George Patterson (82) is being assessed for residential care following a hospital admission. He lives alone and is struggling to manage independently.

**Narrative:**
Social worker Rebecca Williams receives a referral for Mr. Patterson, who is medically fit for discharge but cannot return home safely. She accesses the Core Information Standard to review his comprehensive assessment.

The system shows:
- Recent occupational therapy assessment indicating inability to manage stairs
- No family support (widower, no children)
- Mild depression following wife's death last year
- Type 2 diabetes requiring twice-daily insulin
- Previous good engagement with services
- Receives attendance allowance
- Owner-occupier of 3-bedroom house

Rebecca conducts a mental capacity assessment, which Mr. Patterson passes. Together they complete the 'About Me' section, where he expresses:
- Preference to stay in the local area
- Enjoys reading and classical music
- Vegetarian for 40 years
- Would like a room with garden view
- Anxious about losing independence

The care home manager, Patricia Brown, receives the electronic referral with full information, enabling appropriate room allocation and care planning before admission.

**Data Elements Utilized:**

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Assessment | Functional assessments, mental capacity, social circumstances |
| Preferences | About Me section, dietary requirements, lifestyle choices |
| Clinical | Medical conditions, medication needs, mobility status |
| Financial | Benefits information, property ownership |
| Legal | Capacity assessment, consent for placement |

**Personas Involved:**
- P005: Social Worker (assessment and coordination)
- P007: Care Home Manager (receiving information)
- P008: Patient (active participation)
- P003: Hospital Doctor (medical summary)

**Related Business Processes:**
- BP05: Social Care Assessment
- BP06: Mental Capacity Assessment
- BP07: Care Transition Planning
- BP08: Information Sharing

## Scenario 3: Community Nursing Visit

### Title: District Nurse Home Visit for Wound Care

**Context:** David Lee, a district nurse, visits Mrs. Amelia Singh (68) for daily wound dressing following leg ulcer treatment.

**Narrative:**
David prepares for his morning visits by syncing his tablet with the latest patient information. For Mrs. Singh, the system shows:
- Wound photos from yesterday showing improvement
- New antibiotics started by GP yesterday
- Daughter now staying to help (updated contact)
- Dog warning - must be secured before entry
- Prefers female nurses but accepts male staff
- Speaks Punjabi primarily, daughter interprets

Arriving at the property, David uses the keysafe code from the system. Mrs. Singh's daughter greets him and secures the dog. During the visit, David:
- Photographs the wound for progress monitoring
- Updates the wound assessment template
- Notes Mrs. Singh seems low in mood
- Records that daughter is managing medications well
- Identifies need for interpreter at next GP appointment

The information immediately syncs when back online, alerting the GP to the mood concerns.

**Data Elements Utilized:**

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Clinical | Wound assessments, medication changes, mood observation |
| Access | Keysafe code, dog warning, entry instructions |
| Communication | Language needs, interpreter requirement |
| Contacts | Updated daughter's details, GP for alerts |
| Preferences | Gender preference for care, cultural needs |

**Personas Involved:**
- P006: Community Nurse (direct care delivery)
- P009: Informal Carer (daughter supporting)
- P001: GP (receiving alerts)
- P008: Patient (care recipient)

**Related Business Processes:**
- BP09: Community Care Delivery
- BP10: Mobile Working
- BP11: Clinical Photography
- BP12: Alert Management

## Scenario 4: Annual Health Check

### Title: Learning Disability Annual Health Check in General Practice

**Context:** James Thompson, a practice nurse, conducts an annual health check for Simon Williams (34), who has moderate learning disability.

**Narrative:**
Simon attends with his support worker for his annual health check. The system flags:
- Reasonable adjustments: needs easy-read materials, double appointments, preparation visit offered
- Previous difficult experience with blood tests
- Responds well to clear explanations and choices
- Lives in supported accommodation
- Works part-time at local garden center
- No capacity concerns for routine healthcare

James uses the structured template, which automatically adjusts for Simon's needs:
- Easy-read consent forms appear
- Simplified health questionnaire loads
- Visual pain scale available
- Desensitization protocol for blood tests flagged

During the check, James:
- Uses pictures to explain procedures
- Allows Simon to handle equipment first
- Records Simon's report of feeling tired lately
- Notes weight gain of 5kg since last year
- Updates that Simon now self-administers medication
- Books follow-up for blood tests with preparation visit

**Data Elements Utilized:**

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Adjustments | Communication needs, appointment requirements, preparation protocols |
| Clinical | Health measurements, medication management, symptoms |
| Social | Employment status, living situation, support networks |
| Preferences | How to explain procedures, coping strategies |

**Personas Involved:**
- P002: Practice Nurse (conducting check)
- P008: Patient (active participant)
- Support Worker (attending but not documented persona)
- P001: GP (reviewing results)

**Related Business Processes:**
- BP13: Structured Health Assessments
- BP14: Reasonable Adjustments Implementation
- BP15: Easy-Read Documentation
- BP16: Capacity-Based Consent

## Scenario 5: Multi-Disciplinary Team Meeting

### Title: Hospital Discharge Planning for Complex Patient

**Context:** A multi-disciplinary team meets to plan discharge for Mr. Robert Thompson (79), who was admitted with pneumonia but has complex ongoing needs.

**Narrative:**
The team gathers in the discharge planning meeting room, with some members joining virtually. The ward sister presents via the shared screen showing the Core Information Standard record:

**Medical status:**
- Pneumonia resolving, still on IV antibiotics
- New diagnosis of mild cognitive impairment
- Previous stroke with left-sided weakness
- Polypharmacy - 12 regular medications

**Social situation:**
- Lives with wife who has her own health issues
- Daughter lives nearby but works full-time
- Previously independent with personal care
- Two-story house with bathroom upstairs

**Current assessments:**
- Physiotherapy: Mobility decreased, recommends stairlift
- OT: Needs equipment and possible adaptation
- Nursing: Requires prompting with medication
- Social work: Wife struggling to cope, needs support

The team collaboratively updates the discharge plan in real-time:
- Intermediate care placement for 2 weeks
- Fast-track OT home assessment
- Carer's assessment for wife
- Community nursing for IV antibiotics
- Memory clinic referral

**Data Elements Utilized:**

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Assessments | Multi-professional assessments and recommendations |
| Care Planning | Discharge plan, follow-up arrangements |
| Social | Family dynamics, housing situation, carer needs |
| Clinical | Diagnosis, treatment, medication changes |
| Referrals | New referrals generated from meeting |

**Personas Involved:**
- P004: Ward Nurse (presenting case)
- P003: Hospital Doctor (medical overview)
- P005: Social Worker (discharge coordination)
- Physiotherapist (mobility assessment)
- Occupational Therapist (home assessment)

**Related Business Processes:**
- BP17: Multi-Disciplinary Team Meetings
- BP18: Discharge Planning
- BP19: Complex Care Coordination
- BP20: Real-time Documentation

title: Business Processes & Workflows
content:
## Overview

This section details the key business processes and workflows required to implement the Core Information Standard. Each process includes objectives, workflow elements, and integration points with the standard's data model.

## BP01: Patient Registration and Verification
**Process ID:** BP01

### Objectives
- Ensure accurate patient identification and prevent duplicate records
- Capture mandatory demographic information
- Establish foundation for longitudinal health record
- Enable secure information sharing across care settings

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP01-SE01 | Patient presents for registration |
| User Task | BP01-UT01 | Search existing records |
| Gateway | BP01-GW01 | Record found? |
| User Task | BP01-UT02 | Verify patient identity |
| User Task | BP01-UT03 | Create new patient record |
| User Task | BP01-UT04 | Capture demographic details |
| User Task | BP01-UT05 | Record contact information |
| User Task | BP01-UT06 | Capture GP details |
| System Task | BP01-ST01 | Validate NHS number |
| System Task | BP01-ST02 | Check for duplicates |
| End Event | BP01-EE01 | Registration complete |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP01-SE01 | BP01-UT01 | Always |
| BP01-UT01 | BP01-GW01 | Search completed |
| BP01-GW01 | BP01-UT02 | Record found = Yes |
| BP01-GW01 | BP01-UT03 | Record found = No |
| BP01-UT02 | BP01-UT04 | Identity verified |
| BP01-UT03 | BP01-UT04 | New record created |
| BP01-UT04 | BP01-UT05 | Demographics captured |
| BP01-UT05 | BP01-UT06 | Contact info recorded |
| BP01-UT06 | BP01-ST01 | GP details entered |
| BP01-ST01 | BP01-ST02 | NHS number validated |
| BP01-ST02 | BP01-EE01 | No duplicates found |

## BP02: Clinical Assessment Documentation
**Process ID:** BP02

### Objectives
- Standardize clinical information capture across all settings
- Ensure comprehensive assessment of health status
- Support clinical decision-making with complete information
- Enable continuity of care through consistent documentation

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP02-SE01 | Clinical encounter begins |
| User Task | BP02-UT01 | Review existing information |
| User Task | BP02-UT02 | Conduct clinical assessment |
| User Task | BP02-UT03 | Document vital signs |
| User Task | BP02-UT04 | Update problem list |
| User Task | BP02-UT05 | Review medications |
| Gateway | BP02-GW01 | New diagnosis? |
| User Task | BP02-UT06 | Add diagnosis with SNOMED code |
| User Task | BP02-UT07 | Document examination findings |
| System Task | BP02-ST01 | Clinical validation checks |
| System Task | BP02-ST02 | Generate clinical alerts |
| End Event | BP02-EE01 | Assessment documented |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP02-SE01 | BP02-UT01 | Always |
| BP02-UT01 | BP02-UT02 | Review complete |
| BP02-UT02 | BP02-UT03 | Assessment performed |
| BP02-UT03 | BP02-UT04 | Vitals recorded |
| BP02-UT04 | BP02-UT05 | Problem list updated |
| BP02-UT05 | BP02-GW01 | Medications reviewed |
| BP02-GW01 | BP02-UT06 | New diagnosis = Yes |
| BP02-GW01 | BP02-UT07 | New diagnosis = No |
| BP02-UT06 | BP02-UT07 | Diagnosis added |
| BP02-UT07 | BP02-ST01 | Findings documented |
| BP02-ST01 | BP02-ST02 | Validation passed |
| BP02-ST02 | BP02-EE01 | Alerts generated |

## BP03: Medication Management
**Process ID:** BP03

### Objectives
- Maintain accurate medication records across care transitions
- Prevent medication errors through systematic reconciliation
- Support safe prescribing with allergy and interaction checking
- Enable patient medication adherence through clear documentation

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP03-SE01 | Medication review triggered |
| User Task | BP03-UT01 | Access current medication list |
| User Task | BP03-UT02 | Verify with patient/carer |
| Gateway | BP03-GW01 | Discrepancies found? |
| User Task | BP03-UT03 | Reconcile medications |
| User Task | BP03-UT04 | Check allergies/interactions |
| Gateway | BP03-GW02 | New prescription needed? |
| User Task | BP03-UT05 | Create prescription |
| System Task | BP03-ST01 | Clinical decision support |
| System Task | BP03-ST02 | Send to pharmacy |
| User Task | BP03-UT06 | Document changes |
| End Event | BP03-EE01 | Medication record updated |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP03-SE01 | BP03-UT01 | Always |
| BP03-UT01 | BP03-UT02 | List retrieved |
| BP03-UT02 | BP03-GW01 | Verification complete |
| BP03-GW01 | BP03-UT03 | Discrepancies = Yes |
| BP03-GW01 | BP03-UT04 | Discrepancies = No |
| BP03-UT03 | BP03-UT04 | Reconciliation done |
| BP03-UT04 | BP03-GW02 | Checks complete |
| BP03-GW02 | BP03-UT05 | Prescription = Yes |
| BP03-GW02 | BP03-UT06 | Prescription = No |
| BP03-UT05 | BP03-ST01 | Prescription created |
| BP03-ST01 | BP03-ST02 | CDS checks passed |
| BP03-ST02 | BP03-UT06 | Pharmacy notified |
| BP03-UT06 | BP03-EE01 | Documentation complete |

## BP04: Safeguarding Workflow
**Process ID:** BP04

### Objectives
- Identify and document safeguarding concerns promptly
- Ensure appropriate escalation and multi-agency coordination
- Maintain confidentiality while enabling necessary information sharing
- Support vulnerable individuals through systematic assessment

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP04-SE01 | Safeguarding concern identified |
| User Task | BP04-UT01 | Document initial concern |
| Gateway | BP04-GW01 | Immediate risk? |
| User Task | BP04-UT02 | Initiate emergency protocol |
| User Task | BP04-UT03 | Conduct risk assessment |
| User Task | BP04-UT04 | Check existing alerts |
| System Task | BP04-ST01 | Generate safeguarding flag |
| User Task | BP04-UT05 | Create safeguarding plan |
| User Task | BP04-UT06 | Notify relevant agencies |
| System Task | BP04-ST02 | Secure documentation |
| End Event | BP04-EE01 | Safeguarding recorded |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP04-SE01 | BP04-UT01 | Always |
| BP04-UT01 | BP04-GW01 | Concern documented |
| BP04-GW01 | BP04-UT02 | Immediate risk = Yes |
| BP04-GW01 | BP04-UT03 | Immediate risk = No |
| BP04-UT02 | BP04-UT03 | Emergency addressed |
| BP04-UT03 | BP04-UT04 | Assessment complete |
| BP04-UT04 | BP04-ST01 | History reviewed |
| BP04-ST01 | BP04-UT05 | Flag generated |
| BP04-UT05 | BP04-UT06 | Plan created |
| BP04-UT06 | BP04-ST02 | Agencies notified |
| BP04-ST02 | BP04-EE01 | Documentation secured |

## BP05: Care Planning Process
**Process ID:** BP05

### Objectives
- Develop person-centered care plans with individual involvement
- Coordinate multi-professional input into integrated plans
- Set measurable goals with regular review cycles
- Ensure plans are accessible across care settings

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP05-SE01 | Care planning initiated |
| User Task | BP05-UT01 | Review assessments |
| User Task | BP05-UT02 | Engage person/carer |
| User Task | BP05-UT03 | Identify strengths/needs |
| User Task | BP05-UT04 | Set goals with person |
| User Task | BP05-UT05 | Define interventions |
| Gateway | BP05-GW01 | Multi-disciplinary input needed? |
| User Task | BP05-UT06 | Coordinate team input |
| User Task | BP05-UT07 | Agree review schedule |
| System Task | BP05-ST01 | Link related plans |
| System Task | BP05-ST02 | Set review reminders |
| End Event | BP05-EE01 | Care plan activated |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP05-SE01 | BP05-UT01 | Always |
| BP05-UT01 | BP05-UT02 | Assessments reviewed |
| BP05-UT02 | BP05-UT03 | Person engaged |
| BP05-UT03 | BP05-UT04 | Needs identified |
| BP05-UT04 | BP05-UT05 | Goals agreed |
| BP05-UT05 | BP05-GW01 | Interventions defined |
| BP05-GW01 | BP05-UT06 | MDT input = Yes |
| BP05-GW01 | BP05-UT07 | MDT input = No |
| BP05-UT06 | BP05-UT07 | Team input received |
| BP05-UT07 | BP05-ST01 | Schedule set |
| BP05-ST01 | BP05-ST02 | Plans linked |
| BP05-ST02 | BP05-EE01 | Reminders configured |

## BP06: Information Sharing Workflow
**Process ID:** BP06

### Objectives
- Enable secure, appropriate information sharing between providers
- Respect patient consent and confidentiality requirements
- Support integrated care through timely information exchange
- Maintain audit trails of all information access and sharing

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP06-SE01 | Information request received |
| System Task | BP06-ST01 | Verify requester identity |
| Gateway | BP06-GW01 | Legitimate relationship? |
| User Task | BP06-UT01 | Check consent status |
| Gateway | BP06-GW02 | Consent given? |
| User Task | BP06-UT02 | Apply information filters |
| System Task | BP06-ST02 | Redact sensitive data |
| System Task | BP06-ST03 | Package information |
| System Task | BP06-ST04 | Transmit securely |
| System Task | BP06-ST05 | Log access event |
| User Task | BP06-UT03 | Handle consent override |
| End Event | BP06-EE01 | Information shared |
| End Event | BP06-EE02 | Request denied |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP06-SE01 | BP06-ST01 | Always |
| BP06-ST01 | BP06-GW01 | Identity verified |
| BP06-GW01 | BP06-UT01 | Legitimate = Yes |
| BP06-GW01 | BP06-EE02 | Legitimate = No |
| BP06-UT01 | BP06-GW02 | Consent checked |
| BP06-GW02 | BP06-UT02 | Consent = Yes |
| BP06-GW02 | BP06-UT03 | Consent = No |
| BP06-UT02 | BP06-ST02 | Filters applied |
| BP06-ST02 | BP06-ST03 | Data redacted |
| BP06-ST03 | BP06-ST04 | Package created |
| BP06-ST04 | BP06-ST05 | Transmitted |
| BP06-ST05 | BP06-EE01 | Access logged |
| BP06-UT03 | BP06-EE02 | Override denied |

title: Core Data Elements
content:
## Overview

This section provides detailed specifications for all data elements within the Core Information Standard v2.0. Elements are organized by functional domain and include data types, validation rules, terminology bindings, and linkages to business processes.

## Person Demographics

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| DEM-001 | NHS Number | Unique identifier for person within NHS England/Wales | String | M | 10 digits, modulus 11 check | BP01, BP06 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| DEM-002 | Person First Name | First name(s) including middle names | String | M | Max 35 chars, UTF-8 | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| DEM-003 | Person Family Name | Family name or surname | String | M | Max 35 chars, UTF-8 | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| DEM-004 | Person Preferred Name | Name person wishes to be called | String | R | Max 70 chars | BP01, BP05 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| DEM-005 | Title | Person's title (Mr, Mrs, Dr, etc.) | String | R | Max 35 chars | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| DEM-006 | Date of Birth | Person's date of birth | Date | M | Format: YYYY-MM-DD, not future | BP01, All | NHS Data Dictionary |
| DEM-007 | Gender | Person's stated gender | String | R | Valid codes: M, F, X | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| DEM-008 | Sex | Person's phenotypic sex | String | R | Valid codes: 1, 2, 9 | BP02 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| DEM-009 | Ethnicity | Ethnic category as specified by person | String | R | NHS ethnicity codes | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| DEM-010 | Religion | Religious affiliation | String | R | SNOMED subset | BP01 | SNOMED 999000531000000100 |
| DEM-011 | Marital Status | Legal marital status | String | R | Valid codes: S, M, D, W, P | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| DEM-012 | Photograph | Photo for identification | Binary | R | JPEG/PNG, max 5MB | BP01 | DICOM |
| DEM-013 | NHS Number Status | Reliability indicator for NHS number | String | R | Valid codes: 01-08 | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |

## Address Information

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ADD-001 | Address Line 1 | First line of address | String | M | Max 35 chars | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| ADD-002 | Address Line 2 | Second line of address | String | R | Max 35 chars | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| ADD-003 | Address Line 3 | Third line of address | String | R | Max 35 chars | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| ADD-004 | Address Line 4 | Fourth line of address | String | R | Max 35 chars | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| ADD-005 | Address Line 5 | Fifth line of address | String | R | Max 35 chars | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| ADD-006 | Postcode | UK postcode | String | R | Valid UK postcode format | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| ADD-007 | Address Type | Type of address | String | R | Valid codes: 1-9 | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |

## Contact Information

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CON-001 | Preferred Contact Method | How person prefers to be contacted | String | R | Valid codes: 1-8 | BP01, BP05 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| CON-002 | Telephone Number | Person's phone number | String | R | Max 35 chars, E.164 format | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| CON-003 | Telephone Type | Type of phone (home, mobile, etc.) | String | R | FHIR ContactPointUse | BP01 | FHIR |
| CON-004 | Email Address | Person's email | String | R | Valid email format, max 255 | BP01 | NHS Data Dictionary |
| CON-005 | Email Type | Type of email (personal, work) | String | R | FHIR ContactPointUse | BP01 | FHIR |

## Clinical Information - Problem List

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PRB-001 | Problem/Diagnosis | Clinical problem or diagnosis | Coded | M | Valid SNOMED code | BP02, BP05 | SNOMED 1127581000000103 |
| PRB-002 | Onset Date | When problem began | DateTime | R | Not future date | BP02 | N/A |
| PRB-003 | End Date | When problem resolved | DateTime | R | After onset date | BP02 | N/A |
| PRB-004 | Stage of Disease | Disease stage where relevant | String | R | Max 150 chars | BP02 | Free text |
| PRB-005 | Date Recorded | When added to problem list | DateTime | M | Auto-generated | BP02 | N/A |
| PRB-006 | Recording Professional | Who added the problem | Complex | M | See professional structure | BP02 | Multiple |

## Medications

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| MED-001 | Medication Name | Generic or brand name | Coded | M | Valid dm+d code | BP03 | dm+d (VTM/VMP/AMP) |
| MED-002 | Form | Medication form (tablet, liquid) | Coded | R | dm+d dose forms | BP03 | SNOMED 999000781000001107 |
| MED-003 | Route | Administration route | Coded | R | SNOMED route subset | BP03 | SNOMED 999000051000001100 |
| MED-004 | Dose | Dose amount | Quantity | R | Positive number + units | BP03 | UCUM |
| MED-005 | Frequency | How often taken | String | R | Structured dose syntax | BP03 | HL7 timing |
| MED-006 | Start Date | When medication started | DateTime | R | Not future date | BP03 | N/A |
| MED-007 | End Date | When medication stopped | DateTime | R | After start date | BP03 | N/A |
| MED-008 | Indication | Reason for medication | String | R | Max 150 chars | BP03 | Free text |
| MED-009 | Prescriber | Who prescribed | Complex | M | See professional structure | BP03 | Multiple |
| MED-010 | Status | Active/stopped/suspended | String | M | FHIR MedicationStatus | BP03 | FHIR |

## Allergies and Adverse Reactions

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ALL-001 | Causative Agent | Substance causing reaction | Coded | M | dm+d or SNOMED | BP02, BP03 | Multiple |
| ALL-002 | Type of Reaction | Allergy/intolerance/adverse | String | R | FHIR AllergyType | BP02 | FHIR |
| ALL-003 | Reaction Description | Manifestation of reaction | Coded | R | SNOMED finding | BP02 | SNOMED 1127581000000103 |
| ALL-004 | Severity | Severity of reaction | String | R | FHIR AllergySeverity | BP02 | FHIR |
| ALL-005 | Certainty | Certainty of diagnosis | String | R | FHIR AllergyCertainty | BP02 | FHIR |
| ALL-006 | Date Recorded | When allergy recorded | DateTime | M | Auto-generated | BP02 | N/A |
| ALL-007 | Recorder | Who recorded allergy | Complex | M | See professional structure | BP02 | Multiple |

## Individual Requirements

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| REQ-001 | Reasonable Adjustment Flag | Has reasonable adjustments | Boolean | R | Yes/No | BP01, All | SNOMED 1108111000000107 |
| REQ-002 | Adjustment Type | Type of adjustment needed | Coded | R | SNOMED subset | BP01, All | SNOMED 999002121000000109 |
| REQ-003 | Adjustment Details | Specific requirements | String | R | Max 500 chars | BP01, All | Free text |
| REQ-004 | Communication Needs | Communication support required | Coded | R | SNOMED subset | BP01, All | SNOMED 999002151000000104 |
| REQ-005 | Mobility Needs | Mobility support required | Coded | R | SNOMED subset | BP01, All | SNOMED 999002551000000108 |
| REQ-006 | Cognitive Support | Cognitive support needs | String | R | Max 150 chars | BP01, BP05 | Free text |

## Safeguarding

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SAF-001 | Safeguarding Flag | Active safeguarding concerns | Boolean | R | Yes/No | BP04 | N/A |
| SAF-002 | Safeguarding Type | Type of concern | Coded | R | SNOMED subset | BP04 | SNOMED 999002381000000108 |
| SAF-003 | Date Identified | When concern identified | DateTime | M | Not future date | BP04 | N/A |
| SAF-004 | Risk Level | Level of risk | String | R | High/Medium/Low | BP04 | Local |
| SAF-005 | Safeguarding Plan | Active plan details | String | R | Max 500 chars | BP04 | Free text |
| SAF-006 | Lead Professional | Safeguarding lead | Complex | R | See professional structure | BP04 | Multiple |

## Legal Information

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| LEG-001 | Mental Capacity Status | Capacity for decisions | String | R | Has capacity/Lacks capacity | BP05, BP06 | Local |
| LEG-002 | LPA Health Welfare | Lasting power of attorney | Boolean | R | Yes/No | BP05, BP06 | SNOMED 999001951000000107 |
| LEG-003 | LPA Contact Details | Attorney contact info | Complex | R | Name + contact | BP05, BP06 | Multiple |
| LEG-004 | DNACPR Status | Do not resuscitate decision | String | R | For CPR/Not for CPR | BP05 | SNOMED 450475007/450476008 |
| LEG-005 | ADRT Status | Advance decision status | Boolean | R | Yes/No | BP05 | SNOMED 816281000000101 |
| LEG-006 | Deprivation of Liberty | DoLS status | String | R | Requested/Authorised | BP04, BP05 | Local |

## Care Planning

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CPL-001 | Care Plan Type | Type of care plan | Coded | R | SNOMED subset | BP05 | SNOMED 999001471000000108 |
| CPL-002 | Goals | Person's goals | String | R | Max 500 chars | BP05 | Free text |
| CPL-003 | Actions | Planned actions | Complex | R | What/Who/When structure | BP05 | Multiple |
| CPL-004 | Review Date | Next review date | Date | R | Future date | BP05 | N/A |
| CPL-005 | Agreed With Person | Person agreement status | Boolean | M | Yes/No | BP05 | N/A |
| CPL-006 | Care Coordinator | Lead professional | Complex | R | See professional structure | BP05 | Multiple |

title: Decision-Support Logic
content:
## Overview

This section defines the decision-support logic embedded within the Core Information Standard implementation. These decision tables guide system behavior, clinical alerts, validation rules, and workflow routing based on the core data elements.

## DT01: Patient Identity Verification
### Title: Identity Matching and Verification Rules
### Description: Determines the level of identity verification required and actions to take based on available identifiers

**Input Elements:**
- NHS Number (DEM-001)
- First Name (DEM-002)
- Family Name (DEM-003)
- Date of Birth (DEM-006)
- Postcode (ADD-006)

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | NHS Number Present | NHS Number Valid | Name Match | DOB Match | Postcode Match | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C01.1 | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Allow full access | High confidence match |
| C01.2 | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | Confirm address change | Possible recent move |
| C01.3 | Yes | Yes | No | Yes | Yes | Flag name discrepancy | Possible name change |
| C01.4 | Yes | No | - | - | - | Block access, investigate | Invalid NHS number |
| C01.5 | No | - | Yes | Yes | Yes | Create temporary ID | New registration |
| C01.6 | No | - | Partial | Yes | No | Manual verification required | Insufficient match |
| C01.7 | No | - | No | No | No | Deny access | No match found |

## DT02: Clinical Alert Generation
### Title: Automated Clinical Alert Rules
### Description: Generates appropriate clinical alerts based on patient data patterns and risk indicators

**Input Elements:**
- Age (calculated from DEM-006)
- Allergies (ALL-001)
- Safeguarding Flag (SAF-001)
- Mental Capacity (LEG-001)
- DNACPR Status (LEG-004)
- Falls Risk (from Problem List)

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | Age >75 | Drug Allergy | Safeguarding | Lacks Capacity | DNACPR | Falls Risk | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C02.1 | Yes | Yes | - | - | - | Yes | Generate high priority alert bundle | Multiple risks |
| C02.2 | - | Yes | - | - | - | - | Display allergy banner | Medication safety |
| C02.3 | - | - | Yes | - | - | - | Restrict access, notify safeguarding lead | Protection required |
| C02.4 | - | - | - | Yes | - | - | Display capacity banner, check LPA | Decision support |
| C02.5 | - | - | - | - | Yes | - | Display DNACPR status prominently | Emergency response |
| C02.6 | Yes | - | - | - | - | Yes | Initiate falls prevention pathway | Preventive care |
| C02.7 | No | No | No | No | No | No | Standard display | No special alerts |

## DT03: Reasonable Adjustment Requirements
### Title: Adjustment Need Identification
### Description: Identifies required adjustments based on patient characteristics and recorded needs

**Input Elements:**
- Communication Needs (REQ-004)
- Mobility Needs (REQ-005)
- Cognitive Support (REQ-006)
- Sensory Impairments (from Problem List)
- Learning Disability (from Problem List)

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | Communication Need | Mobility Need | Cognitive Need | Visual Impairment | Hearing Impairment | Learning Disability | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C03.1 | Interpreter | - | - | - | - | - | Book interpreter, allow double appointment | Language support |
| C03.2 | - | Wheelchair | - | - | - | - | Ensure accessible room, ground floor | Physical access |
| C03.3 | - | - | Yes | - | - | Yes | Provide easy-read materials, allow supporter | Cognitive support |
| C03.4 | - | - | - | Yes | - | - | Large print materials, verbal communication | Visual adjustment |
| C03.5 | - | - | - | - | Yes | - | Book BSL interpreter or use hearing loop | Hearing adjustment |
| C03.6 | Easy read | - | Yes | - | - | Yes | Comprehensive support package | Multiple needs |
| C03.7 | Multiple | Multiple | Multiple | - | - | - | Refer to disability liaison nurse | Complex needs |

## DT04: Medication Safety Checks
### Title: Medication Prescribing Safety Rules
### Description: Validates medication orders against patient data to prevent errors

**Input Elements:**
- Medication to prescribe (MED-001)
- Current medications (MED-001 list)
- Allergies (ALL-001)
- Age (calculated)
- Renal function (from recent labs)
- Pregnancy status

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | Penicillin Allergy | Prescribing Penicillin | Age >75 | eGFR <30 | Pregnancy | Interacting Drug | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C04.1 | Yes | Yes | - | - | - | - | Block prescription | Allergy contraindication |
| C04.2 | - | - | Yes | - | - | Yes | Warning: adjust dose | Elderly + interaction |
| C04.3 | - | - | - | Yes | - | - | Calculate renal dosing | Renal impairment |
| C04.4 | - | - | - | - | Yes | - | Check pregnancy category | Pregnancy safety |
| C04.5 | No | No | Yes | Yes | - | - | Multiple warnings | Multiple factors |
| C04.6 | No | No | No | No | No | No | Allow prescription | No contraindications |

## DT05: Care Transition Requirements
### Title: Information Requirements for Care Transitions
### Description: Determines what information must be shared during care transitions

**Input Elements:**
- Transition Type (admission, discharge, referral)
- Destination Setting (hospital, home, care home)
- Patient Complexity Score
- Active Safeguarding (SAF-001)
- Consent Status

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | Transition Type | To Hospital | To Care Home | To Home | Complex Patient | Safeguarding | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C05.1 | Admission | Yes | - | - | - | - | Send GP summary + medications | Standard admission |
| C05.2 | Discharge | - | Yes | - | Yes | - | Full assessment pack + care plan | Complex to care home |
| C05.3 | Discharge | - | - | Yes | Yes | - | Arrange community follow-up | Complex to home |
| C05.4 | Any | Any | Any | Any | - | Yes | Include safeguarding plan | Protection required |
| C05.5 | Referral | Yes | - | - | - | - | Clinical summary + reason | Standard referral |
| C05.6 | Discharge | - | Yes | - | - | - | Medication list + care needs | Standard to care home |

## DT06: Capacity and Consent Decisions
### Title: Consent Validity and Override Rules
### Description: Determines when consent can be overridden and what documentation is required

**Input Elements:**
- Mental Capacity Status (LEG-001)
- Consent Given (Y/N)
- LPA Status (LEG-002)
- Emergency Status
- Best Interests Assessment

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | Has Capacity | Consent Given | LPA Present | Emergency | Best Interests Done | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C06.1 | Yes | Yes | - | - | - | Proceed with treatment | Standard consent |
| C06.2 | Yes | No | - | No | - | Cannot proceed | Patient refusal |
| C06.3 | Yes | No | - | Yes | - | Treat under emergency | Life-threatening |
| C06.4 | No | - | Yes | - | - | Consult LPA | Attorney decides |
| C06.5 | No | - | No | No | Yes | Proceed per assessment | Best interests |
| C06.6 | No | - | No | Yes | No | Treat, document later | Emergency + no capacity |
| C06.7 | No | - | No | No | No | Cannot proceed | Need assessment |

## DT07: Safeguarding Escalation
### Title: Safeguarding Response Requirements
### Description: Determines escalation requirements based on safeguarding indicators

**Input Elements:**
- Safeguarding Type (SAF-002)
- Risk Level (SAF-004)
- Person Age
- Existing Plan (SAF-005)
- Professional Judgment

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | Child <18 | Vulnerable Adult | Risk Level High | Active Plan | New Concern | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C07.1 | Yes | - | Yes | No | Yes | Immediate referral to children's services | Child protection |
| C07.2 | Yes | - | Medium | Yes | Yes | Update plan, notify social worker | Existing case |
| C07.3 | No | Yes | High | No | Yes | Adult safeguarding referral within 24h | Adult protection |
| C07.4 | No | Yes | Low | No | Yes | Document, monitor, review in 7 days | Low risk |
| C07.5 | - | - | High | Yes | Yes | Immediate case conference | Escalation |
| C07.6 | No | No | Low | No | No | No action required | No concerns |

## DT08: Access Control
### Title: Information Access Permissions
### Description: Determines what information can be accessed based on user role and context

**Input Elements:**
- User Role (from Personas)
- Care Relationship
- Information Sensitivity
- Patient Consent
- Legal Override

### Decision Table

| Condition ID | Direct Care | Emergency Access | Admin Role | Sensitive Data | Consent | Legal Override | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C08.1 | Yes | - | No | No | Yes | - | Full clinical access | Standard care |
| C08.2 | Yes | - | No | Yes | Yes | - | Access with audit | Sensitive + consent |
| C08.3 | No | Yes | No | - | - | Yes | Emergency access only | Life-saving |
| C08.4 | No | No | Yes | No | - | - | Demographics only | Admin access |
| C08.5 | Yes | - | No | Yes | No | No | Restricted access | No consent |
| C08.6 | - | - | - | Yes | No | Yes | Override with documentation | Legal requirement |
| C08.7 | No | No | No | - | - | - | Access denied | No relationship |

title: Indicators & Performance Metrics
content:
## Overview

This section defines key performance indicators (KPIs) and metrics to monitor the implementation and effectiveness of the Core Information Standard. These indicators support quality improvement, regulatory compliance, and outcome measurement.

## Core Implementation Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND-001 | Patient Identity Verification Rate | Number of patients with verified NHS number and 2+ demographic identifiers | Total registered patients | By facility, age group, registration type | Monthly | System audit log |
| IND-002 | Record Completeness - Demographics | Number of patients with all mandatory demographic fields completed | Total registered patients | By facility, registration date | Monthly | Data quality report |
| IND-003 | Contact Information Currency | Number of patients with contact details updated within 12 months | Total active patients | By age group, care setting | Quarterly | System timestamp |
| IND-004 | GP Registration Coverage | Number of patients with recorded GP practice details | Total registered patients | By locality, age group | Monthly | Registration system |

## Clinical Information Quality Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND-005 | Problem List Maintenance | Number of patients with problem list reviewed within 6 months | Total patients with chronic conditions | By condition, clinician, facility | Monthly | Clinical system |
| IND-006 | Allergy Documentation Rate | Number of patients with allergy status documented (including NKDA) | Total registered patients | By age group, care setting | Monthly | Clinical records |
| IND-007 | Medication Reconciliation Timeliness | Number of admissions with medicines reconciled within 24 hours | Total hospital admissions | By ward, admission type | Weekly | Pharmacy system |
| IND-008 | SNOMED Coding Compliance | Number of diagnoses recorded with SNOMED codes | Total diagnoses recorded | By clinician type, facility | Monthly | Clinical system |

## Person-Centered Care Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND-009 | Reasonable Adjustments Identified | Number of patients with reasonable adjustments flagged | Total patients assessed | By adjustment type, disability | Monthly | Equality monitoring |
| IND-010 | About Me Completion | Number of patients with completed "About Me" section | Total patients in long-term care | By care setting, age | Quarterly | Care records |
| IND-011 | Care Plan Agreement | Number of care plans agreed with person/representative | Total care plans created | By setting, capacity status | Monthly | Care planning system |
| IND-012 | Preferred Name Usage | Number of patients with preferred name recorded and used | Total registered patients | By age group, gender | Quarterly | System audit |

## Safeguarding and Legal Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND-013 | Safeguarding Screening Rate | Number of patients screened for safeguarding risks | Total eligible patients | By risk type, age group | Monthly | Safeguarding system |
| IND-014 | Mental Capacity Documentation | Number of relevant decisions with capacity assessment | Total significant decisions made | By decision type, setting | Monthly | Legal records |
| IND-015 | Advance Decision Recording | Number of patients with ADRT/DNACPR status documented | Total patients >65 or with life-limiting illness | By age, diagnosis | Quarterly | Clinical system |
| IND-016 | Consent Documentation Rate | Number of episodes with valid consent recorded | Total treatment episodes | By treatment type, urgency | Monthly | Consent system |

## Safety and Quality Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND-017 | Clinical Alert Response Time | Number of critical alerts acknowledged within 1 hour | Total critical alerts generated | By alert type, time of day | Weekly | Alert system |
| IND-018 | Medication Error Rate | Number of medication errors linked to information gaps | Total medication orders | By error type, stage | Monthly | Incident system |
| IND-019 | Information Transfer Completeness | Number of transfers with complete information package | Total care transfers | By transfer type, destination | Weekly | Transfer audit |
| IND-020 | Duplicate Record Rate | Number of duplicate patient records identified | Total patient records | By creation source | Monthly | MPI system |

## Access and Sharing Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND-021 | Appropriate Access Rate | Number of access events with valid care relationship | Total access events | By user role, time | Daily | Access log |
| IND-022 | Consent Override Justification | Number of override events with complete documentation | Total consent overrides | By reason, user type | Weekly | Audit system |
| IND-023 | Information Sharing Timeliness | Number of discharge summaries sent within 24 hours | Total discharges | By destination, day of week | Daily | Discharge system |
| IND-024 | Cross-Organization Access | Number of successful cross-organization record access | Total access attempts | By organization pair | Monthly | HIE logs |

## User Experience Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND-025 | Patient Portal Adoption | Number of patients actively using portal/app | Total eligible patients | By age, digital literacy | Monthly | Portal analytics |
| IND-026 | Information Accuracy Reports | Number of patient-reported inaccuracies | Total patient reviews | By data type | Monthly | Feedback system |
| IND-027 | Staff System Satisfaction | Staff rating system as easy to use (≥4/5) | Total staff surveyed | By role, department | Quarterly | User survey |
| IND-028 | Documentation Time | Average time to complete standard documentation | N/A | By task type, user role | Monthly | Time tracking |

## Outcome Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND-029 | Care Coordination Effectiveness | Patients reporting well-coordinated care | Total patients surveyed | By condition complexity | Annual | Patient survey |
| IND-030 | Preventable Readmission Rate | Readmissions linked to information gaps | Total readmissions within 30 days | By initial diagnosis | Monthly | Readmission audit |
| IND-031 | Medication Adherence | Patients with improved adherence after reconciliation | Total patients with reconciliation | By number of medications | Quarterly | Pharmacy data |
| IND-032 | Safeguarding Outcome Success | Safeguarding cases with positive outcome | Total closed safeguarding cases | By intervention type | Quarterly | Safeguarding review |

## Regulatory Compliance Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND-033 | Equality Act Compliance | Services meeting all adjustment requirements | Total services audited | By service type | Annual | Compliance audit |
| IND-034 | Information Governance Compliance | IG incidents related to core information | Total IG incidents | By severity, cause | Monthly | IG system |
| IND-035 | Statutory Reporting Completeness | Reports with all required data elements | Total statutory reports | By report type | Monthly | Reporting system |
| IND-036 | Audit Trail Completeness | Records with complete audit trail | Total records accessed | By record type | Weekly | Audit system |

## Implementation Progress Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IND-037 | Staff Training Completion | Staff completing core standard training | Total clinical staff | By role, department | Monthly | Training system |
| IND-038 | System Integration Progress | Data elements successfully mapped | Total required data elements | By source system | Monthly | Integration dashboard |
| IND-039 | Workflow Adoption Rate | Encounters using standard workflows | Total encounters | By workflow type | Weekly | Workflow engine |
| IND-040 | Data Migration Completeness | Historical records successfully migrated | Total historical records | By record age, type | One-time | Migration report |

title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements
content:
## Overview

This section specifies the functional and non-functional requirements for systems implementing the Core Information Standard v2.0. Requirements are mapped to business processes and user personas to ensure comprehensive coverage of all use cases.

## Functional Requirements

### Identity Management Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR-001 | Patient Search and Matching | System shall provide multi-parameter search (NHS number, name, DOB, postcode) with fuzzy matching and duplicate detection algorithms | BP01 | Critical |
| FR-002 | Biometric Capture | System shall support photograph capture and storage for patient identification, with ability to update photos | BP01 | High |
| FR-003 | NHS Number Validation | System shall validate NHS numbers using modulus 11 algorithm and check against national spine when online | BP01 | Critical |
| FR-004 | Demographic History | System shall maintain audit trail of all demographic changes with timestamp and user attribution | BP01 | High |
| FR-005 | Preferred Name Management | System shall capture and prominently display preferred names throughout all interfaces and outputs | BP01, All | High |

### Clinical Documentation Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR-006 | Problem List Management | System shall maintain coded problem list with SNOMED CT, including onset/resolution dates, severity, and status | BP02 | Critical |
| FR-007 | Allergy Recording | System shall enforce structured allergy documentation with causative agent, reaction type, severity, and certainty | BP02, BP03 | Critical |
| FR-008 | Medication Reconciliation | System shall support side-by-side comparison of medication lists from multiple sources with reconciliation workflow | BP03 | Critical |
| FR-009 | Clinical Alerts | System shall generate real-time alerts for allergies, drug interactions, and clinical risks based on patient data | BP02, BP03 | Critical |
| FR-010 | Assessment Templates | System shall provide configurable templates for standardized assessments with mandatory field validation | BP02 | High |

### Care Planning Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR-011 | Goal-Oriented Planning | System shall support creation of person-centered goals with measurable outcomes and target dates | BP05 | High |
| FR-012 | Multi-Disciplinary Input | System shall enable multiple professionals to contribute to single care plan with attribution and versioning | BP05 | High |
| FR-013 | Care Plan Sharing | System shall generate shareable care plan summaries in multiple formats (PDF, HL7, printed) | BP05, BP06 | High |
| FR-014 | Progress Tracking | System shall track progress against care plan goals with visual indicators and trend analysis | BP05 | Medium |
| FR-015 | Review Scheduling | System shall automatically schedule and remind for care plan reviews based on configured intervals | BP05 | Medium |

### Person-Centered Features

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR-016 | About Me Section | System shall provide free-text and structured fields for capturing what matters to the person | BP05 | High |
| FR-017 | Communication Preferences | System shall record and apply communication preferences including language, format, and contact methods | BP01, All | High |
| FR-018 | Reasonable Adjustments | System shall flag required adjustments prominently on all screens with specific guidance for staff | BP01, All | Critical |
| FR-019 | Easy-Read Mode | System shall provide easy-read versions of key information and consent forms when required | All | High |
| FR-020 | Proxy Access | System shall support secure proxy access for carers with appropriate consent controls | BP06 | High |

### Safeguarding and Legal

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR-021 | Safeguarding Alerts | System shall display prominent safeguarding alerts with restricted access to detailed information | BP04 | Critical |
| FR-022 | Capacity Assessment | System shall support structured capacity assessments linked to specific decisions with temporal validity | BP05, BP06 | Critical |
| FR-023 | Consent Management | System shall track consent for information sharing with override capabilities for emergencies | BP06 | Critical |
| FR-024 | Legal Document Registry | System shall maintain registry of legal documents (LPA, ADRT) with quick access and validity status | BP05 | High |
| FR-025 | Confidential Information | System shall support marking and controlling access to sensitive information with break-glass functionality | BP04, BP06 | Critical |

### Information Exchange

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR-026 | Standardized Exports | System shall export data in standard formats (HL7 FHIR, CDA, PDF) for common transfer scenarios | BP06 | Critical |
| FR-027 | Selective Sharing | System shall allow filtering of information for sharing based on relevance and consent | BP06 | High |
| FR-028 | Audit Trail | System shall maintain comprehensive audit trail of all access and sharing events with justification | BP06 | Critical |
| FR-029 | Real-Time Sync | System shall support real-time synchronization with authorized partner systems | BP06 | Medium |
| FR-030 | Offline Capability | System shall function offline with secure local storage and queued synchronization | BP06 | High |

### Workflow Support

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR-031 | Task Management | System shall generate and track tasks based on care plans and clinical rules | BP05 | Medium |
| FR-032 | Escalation Paths | System shall support configurable escalation pathways for clinical and safeguarding concerns | BP04 | High |
| FR-033 | Mobile Workflows | System shall provide mobile-optimized workflows for community and ward-based care | All | High |
| FR-034 | Bulk Operations | System shall support bulk updates for common operations (e.g., ward rounds, clinic lists) | BP02 | Medium |
| FR-035 | Decision Support | System shall provide context-sensitive decision support based on guidelines and patient data | BP02, BP03 | High |

## Non-Functional Requirements

### Performance Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-001 | Response Time | System shall display patient summary within 2 seconds of selection 95% of the time | Performance | Critical |
| NFR-002 | Search Performance | Patient searches shall return results within 1 second for up to 10 million records | Performance | Critical |
| NFR-003 | Concurrent Users | System shall support minimum 1000 concurrent users per facility without degradation | Performance | High |
| NFR-004 | Data Entry Speed | System shall not introduce more than 100ms latency for keyboard/touch input | Performance | High |
| NFR-005 | Bulk Processing | System shall process bulk updates at minimum 100 records per second | Performance | Medium |

### Availability and Reliability

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-006 | System Availability | System shall maintain 99.9% availability during core hours (07:00-22:00) | Availability | Critical |
| NFR-007 | Planned Downtime | Planned maintenance shall not exceed 4 hours per month outside core hours | Availability | High |
| NFR-008 | Data Durability | System shall ensure zero data loss with real-time replication to secondary site | Reliability | Critical |
| NFR-009 | Failover Time | System shall complete failover to backup systems within 30 seconds | Reliability | Critical |
| NFR-010 | Recovery Point | Recovery point objective (RPO) shall not exceed 5 minutes | Reliability | Critical |

### Security Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-011 | Authentication | System shall support multi-factor authentication for all users with smartcard integration | Security | Critical |
| NFR-012 | Encryption | All data shall be encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3+) | Security | Critical |
| NFR-013 | Access Control | System shall enforce role-based access control with legitimate relationship verification | Security | Critical |
| NFR-014 | Session Management | System shall automatically lock sessions after 10 minutes of inactivity | Security | High |
| NFR-015 | Audit Logging | System shall log all data access with user, timestamp, reason, and data viewed | Security | Critical |

### Usability Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-016 | Accessibility | System shall meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards for accessibility | Usability | Critical |
| NFR-017 | Mobile Responsiveness | System shall be fully functional on tablets and smartphones with touch optimization | Usability | High |
| NFR-018 | Training Time | New users shall be able to complete basic tasks after maximum 4 hours training | Usability | High |
| NFR-019 | Error Messages | System shall provide clear, actionable error messages with suggested resolutions | Usability | High |
| NFR-020 | Multilingual Support | System shall support minimum 10 most common languages in the service area | Usability | Medium |

### Scalability Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-021 | Horizontal Scaling | System shall support horizontal scaling to accommodate 100% growth without redesign | Scalability | High |
| NFR-022 | Data Volume | System shall maintain performance with up to 50 million patient records | Scalability | High |
| NFR-023 | Document Storage | System shall support minimum 1GB storage per patient for documents and images | Scalability | Medium |
| NFR-024 | Transaction Volume | System shall handle peak loads of 10,000 transactions per minute | Scalability | High |
| NFR-025 | Historical Data | System shall maintain 10 years of historical data with sub-second access | Scalability | Medium |

### Integration Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-026 | API Standards | System shall provide FHIR R4 compliant APIs for all core resources | Integration | Critical |
| NFR-027 | Legacy Support | System shall support HL7 v2.x messaging for legacy system integration | Integration | High |
| NFR-028 | Terminology Services | System shall integrate with national terminology services for real-time validation | Integration | High |
| NFR-029 | Identity Services | System shall integrate with national identity services (PDS, CIS) | Integration | Critical |
| NFR-030 | Document Exchange | System shall support XDS.b profile for document sharing | Integration | Medium |

### Compliance Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR-031 | Data Protection | System shall comply with UK GDPR and Data Protection Act 2018 | Compliance | Critical |
| NFR-032 | Clinical Safety | System shall maintain DCB0129 and DCB0160 clinical safety compliance | Compliance | Critical |
| NFR-033 | Information Governance | System shall meet NHS Information Governance Toolkit requirements | Compliance | Critical |
| NFR-034 | Equality Act | System shall demonstrate compliance with Equality Act 2010 requirements | Compliance | Critical |
| NFR-035 | Retention Policies | System shall enforce records retention according to NHS Records Management Code | Compliance | High |
